title: The family of Jesus part 3
scripture_text: ‘’Then Jesus entered a house, and again a crowd gathered, so that he and his disciples were not even able to eat. Then his family heard about this, they went to take charge of him, for they said, “He is out of his mind.” Then Jesus’ mother and brothers arrived. Standing outside, they sent someone in to call him. A crowd was sitting around him, and they told him, “Your mother and brothers are outside looking for you.” “Who are my mother and my brothers?” he asked. Then he looked at those seated in a circle around him and said “Here are my mother and my brothers! Whoever does God’s will is my brother and sister and mother.”
devotional:
The gospel writers strongly suggest that the relationships between Jesus and the rest of the family were often strained. And almost every time we see Jesus with Mary there is stress and misunderstanding. There are four recorded incidents where Jesus and his mother appear together.

When Jesus was a boy of 12 years old, his mother and father had to come looking for him; 'When they found Him Jesus said to them "Didn't you know I had to be in my Father's house?" but they did not understand what he was saying to them." Luke 2:49-50

At the wedding in Cana we read "When the wine was gone, Jesus' mother said to him. ‘They have no more wine." "Dear woman, why do you involve me?" Jesus said. "My time has not yet come." John 2:34.

The third incident is the one we have looked in Mark 3:20-21,31-35, Matthew 12:46-50.

The last incident is when Jesus was dying on the cross. "When Jesus saw his mother there, and the disciple who he loved standing nearby, he said to his mother. "Dear woman, here is your son." And to the disciple. "Here is your mother." From that time on that disciple took her into his home." John 19:26-27

The first three incidents have something in common. Jesus made reference to his Father's will. As His mother, perhaps Mary's biggest struggle was surrendering Jesus to the will of his heavenly Father. It had been a long time since Mary had prayed "l’m the Lord's servant. May it be to me as you have said." Jesus was now thirty years old, Mary would have been in her mid forties, and Joseph was probably dead. How could she let him go? She needed him. Jesus understood this, but he was totally committed to his Father.

The arrival at the house.

Jesus' family leave their home and arrive at the house where Jesus was staying. They send someone in to tell him that they, his family wanted him outside. In response Jesus says ' 'Who are my mother and brothers?' He asked. Then he looked at those seated in a circle around him and said. '’Here are my mother and brothers! Whoever does God's will is my brother and sister and mother."

To help us understand what Jesus meant when he said these words, we must keep in mind the whole context of this incident. Jesus was under great pressure, he had been accused of being demonised, and His family thought he was crazy, so he must have been feeling lonely and vulnerable.

The crowds around Jesus would have believed (because of the importance of the family in that culture) that family members have priority relationship over others. But instead of dropping everything and going out to see his family, Jesus gives a response that would have shocked all that were listening. There are a number of lessons we need to learn from Jesus' response. Jesus draws a contrast between his natural family and his Spiritual family. There are two ways we can relate to Jesus; we either relate to him naturally or spiritually. When we relate to Jesus at a natural level, we will be like his family, we will try and control him, we will expect him to come to us, our communication with him will be all about us trying to control and manipulate him. On the other hand, to relate to Jesus spiritually is to have a submissive heart that bows to the will of his Father.

Enjoying God as father and doing his will increases a person capacity to relate to Jesus. We cannot live in devotion to the Father and not have intimacy with Jesus.

The family of God is made up of those who live under the same authority as Jesus Christ. We cannot be committed to Jesus until we are as committed as Jesus is.

Jesus finds his fellowship and acceptance with those who do the will of his father. At this vulnerable time in Jesus' life, he needed those who would understand and accept him. Only those who have a heart set on doing the Fathers will can accept Jesus and what he stands for. When Jesus pointed out to those seated around him, He was expressing his hearts desire for real intimacy with those who are called disciples.
prayer: Father help me to understand my family better.
html:
The family of Jesus part 3

Study Text: Mark 3:20,21,31-34
‘’Then Jesus entered a house, and again a crowd gathered, so that he and his disciples were not even able to eat. Then his family heard about this, they went to take charge of him, for they said, “He is out of his mind.” Then Jesus’ mother and brothers arrived. Standing outside, they sent someone in to call him. A crowd was sitting around him, and they told him, “Your mother and brothers are outside looking for you.” “Who are my mother and my brothers?” he asked. Then he looked at those seated in a circle around him and said “Here are my mother and my brothers! Whoever does God’s will is my brother and sister and mother.”

The gospel writers strongly suggest that the relationships between Jesus and the rest of the family were often strained. And almost every time we see Jesus with Mary there is stress and misunderstanding. There are four recorded incidents where Jesus and his mother appear together.

When Jesus was a boy of 12 years old, his mother and father had to come looking for him; 'When they found Him Jesus said to them "Didn't you know I had to be in my Father's house?" but they did not understand what he was saying to them." Luke 2:49-50

May it be to me as you have said." Jesus was now thirty years old, Mary would have been in her mid forties, and Joseph was probably dead. How could she let him go? She needed him. Jesus finds his fellowship and acceptance with those who do the will of his father. At this vulnerable time in Jesus' life, he needed those who would understand and accept him. Only those who have a heart set on doing the Fathers will can accept Jesus and what he stands for. When Jesus pointed out to those seated around him, He was expressing his hearts desire for real intimacy with those who are called disciples.

Prayer: Father help me to understand my family better.
Bible in a year reading plan: -1Samuel 20-22
